commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f69ec94e23c39ca095a3dbd85e70e6282f9fa21f)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a provider-owned OpenCode Zen catalog with a 48-row seed, live discovery, dynamic model resolution, routing normalization, shared credential fallback, manifest metadata, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +1617, Tests +350. Total +1967 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and v2026.6.8 lack OpenCode Zen catalog/modelCatalog and dynamic resolver hooks while the sibling OpenCode Go provider has them, matching the canonical issue and duplicate onboarding reports.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs provider-owner review and external proof because the remaining blockers are catalog/auth policy plus successful live MiniMax evidence, not a safe automation-only repair.

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause provider catalog and routing fix for the bundled `opencode` Zen provider.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: Medium — the MiniMax route defect identified by ClawSweeper is fixed at the provider-catalog and manifest boundaries, with focused regression coverage. The requested `minimax-m2.7` after-fix live completion is not satisfied locally because the available OpenCode token is rejected by Zen as an invalid API key.
- Restore a plugin-owned OpenCode Zen model catalog for the bundled `opencode` provider.
- Add static rows for the 48 models currently advertised by Zen, plus runtime live discovery for `/zen/v1/models` when credentials are available.
- Restore dynamic model resolution, catalog augmentation, family-specific transport routing, and manifest metadata for manifest-first flows.
- Update MiniMax Zen rows to the Anthropic Messages route so `minimax-m2.7`, `minimax-m2.5`, and `minimax-m3-free` are no longer advertised on the failing OpenAI-compatible path.

Fixes #92479

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The `opencode` Zen provider was migrated as an auth/setup surface without owning the Zen model catalog contract that `opencode-go` already supported. As a result, the provider registry had no source-of-truth model rows, no dynamic resolver for `opencode/<model>` references, and no family-aware transport mapping for Zen model IDs.
- Additional MiniMax root cause from review: MiniMax Zen rows were still classified into the fallback `openai-completions` transport with `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1`, while Zen MiniMax needs the Anthropic-compatible route.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch restores the missing provider-owned catalog/resolver boundary instead of adding downstream string special cases. Runtime catalog loading now resolves real Zen rows when discovery succeeds, keeps a local seed available when discovery is unavailable, and maps each model family at the provider boundary before the generic LLM adapters run.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users who configure OpenCode Zen can select advertised Zen models directly instead of hand-registering every model or seeing `opencode/<model>` fail as unknown/unavailable.
- What did NOT change: This does not change non-OpenCode providers, does not change `opencode-go` runtime behavior, and does not expand the generic LLM adapter APIs outside this provider's catalog/routing contract.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The provider plugin is the source-of-truth owner for bundled provider catalog metadata. `check-upstream-fixed` found main does not already cover this PR, and the remaining product decision is seed scope/ownership, not a competing implementation.
- Patch quality notes: The patch-quality warnings are expected for this provider-catalog repair: the static seed is a deliberate offline guard, default/undefined returns are catalog parsing boundaries, and the larger manifest diff is generated catalog metadata backed by provider proof rather than unrelated refactor.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The bundled `opencode` Zen provider exposed auth/setup but did not expose a model catalog or dynamic model resolver, so `opencode/<model>` selections failed as unknown or unavailable. This patch restores the provider-owned Zen catalog, keeps a 48-row seed visible when live discovery is unavailable, resolves shared `opencode-go` credentials for the Zen catalog, and routes Zen model families through the expected OpenClaw adapters.

**Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at current PR worktree, redacted local OpenCode auth material available from `~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json` but rejected by Zen as an invalid API key for `x-api-key` Anthropic Messages calls.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
source "$EVIDENCE_DIR/context.env"
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec tsx \
  "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof.mts" \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof-after-sync-20260618.log" 2>&1

NO_COLOR=1 CI=1 corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec vitest run \
  extensions/opencode/index.test.ts \
  --testNamePattern "keeps OpenCode Zen catalog coverage aligned with the curated seed" \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-index-minimax-route-after-sync-20260618.log" 2>&1
```

**Evidence after fix:** The current manifest row for `minimax-m2.7` resolves to `anthropic-messages` and `https://opencode.ai/zen`, and the live proof script sends the after-fix request to the Anthropic Messages endpoint `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/messages` with the API key redacted.

```text
OpenCode Zen MiniMax M2.7 after-fix live proof
timestamp=2026-06-18T14:14:41.402Z
manifest_model=minimax-m2.7
manifest_api=anthropic-messages
manifest_baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen
request_url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/messages
request_headers=content-type: application/json; anthropic-version: 2023-06-01; x-api-key: [REDACTED]
request_body={"model":"minimax-m2.7","max_tokens":64,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Reply exactly with OPENCLAW_MINIMAX_ROUTE_OK."}]}
response_status=401
response_ok=false
response_error={"message":"Error from provider: The API key you provided is invalid.","param":null,"code":"UNAUTHORIZED","type":"error"}
```

Previous authenticated proof before this MiniMax route repair: authenticated discovery returned the 48-row Zen catalog with an Authorization header present, the provider catalog resolved using shared `opencode-go` credentials, and representative live completions returned `ok` through Claude/Anthropic Messages, GPT/OpenAI Responses, Gemini/Google Generative AI, Qwen/Anthropic Messages, and MiniMax M2.5/OpenAI-compatible paths.

```text
OpenCode Zen live proof at 2026-06-13T03:27:09.724Z
credential=present (redacted)
authenticated_discovery_models=48
authenticated_discovery_request url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models authorization_header=present status=200
shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48
live_request family=Claude model=claude-haiku-4-5 api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=claude-haiku-4-5 stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=GPT model=gpt-5-nano api=openai-responses baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=gpt-5-nano stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Gemini model=gemini-3-flash api=google-generative-ai baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=gemini-3-flash stream=undefined max_tokens=undefined stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Qwen model=qwen3.6-plus api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=qwen3.6-plus stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request_attempt family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.7 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 result=failed error="unexpected text: <empty>; content="
live_request family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.5 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=minimax-m2.5 stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
result=passed
```

**Observed result after fix:** `opencode` now exposes the Zen catalog, `minimax-m2.7` is no longer routed through the failed OpenAI-compatible path, and the corrected Anthropic endpoint is reached with redacted credentials. The local environment cannot provide the requested successful `minimax-m2.7` model output because the available OpenCode auth token is rejected by Zen before model execution.

**What was not tested:** A successful after-fix `minimax-m2.7` completion still needs a valid OpenCode Zen API key or the remote real-behavior-proof job's repository secret. This update does not claim `proof: sufficient` for MiniMax live behavior until that proof succeeds.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` plus live evidence script `opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof.mts` stored in the evidence directory outside the repo worktree.
- Scenario locked in: Regression tests assert the Zen catalog exposes the 48 model rows, dynamic model resolution works for Zen IDs, shared `opencode-go` credentials populate the `opencode` catalog path, and Claude/GPT/Gemini/Qwen/MiniMax families route to the expected adapter/base URL pairs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: A focused provider test locks the source-of-truth catalog/routing contract without adding broad adapter mocks, while the live proof attempt covers the external provider boundary far enough to show the corrected MiniMax endpoint and auth failure boundary.

```text
RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-92495

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 9 skipped (10)
Start at  22:13:11
Duration  160.88s
```

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — fix or remove MiniMax Zen rows:** Fixed by routing MiniMax Zen rows through `anthropic-messages` at `https://opencode.ai/zen` rather than removing provider-owned catalog rows. The provider-catalog resolver and manifest rows now agree for `minimax-m2.7`, `minimax-m2.5`, and `minimax-m3-free`.
- **RF-002 — redacted after-fix MiniMax live output:** Redacted after-fix request output is now included above. It shows the corrected Anthropic Messages endpoint and headers, but the local available OpenCode token returns `401 UNAUTHORIZED`, so this does not satisfy successful model-output proof.
- **RF-003 — stronger real behavior proof before merge:** The route-level defect is fixed and the old failed `minimax-m2.7` OpenAI-compatible route is no longer advertised. Successful `minimax-m2.7` live completion remains a proof boundary until run with a valid Zen API key or the remote proof job's secret.
- **RF-004 — failing selectable `minimax-m2.7` row:** The selectable row no longer points at the route that failed in the previous proof. It now points at the Anthropic-compatible route ClawSweeper requested.
- **RF-005 — 48-row static seed drift risk:** The 48-row seed is intentionally provider-owned so Zen remains usable when live discovery is unavailable, while authenticated live discovery is preferred whenever credentials are present. If maintainers want a curated subset instead, that is a seed-scope policy decision rather than a code defect in this patch.
- **RF-006 — shared auth compatibility:** Shared `opencode` / `opencode-go` credentials stay bounded to OpenCode catalog loading. The compatibility risk for existing hand-authored overrides remains disclosed in `## Merge risk`.
- **RF-007 — author/maintainer follow-up:** Author-side route follow-up is implemented. The remaining MiniMax live-output proof requires a valid Zen API key or maintainer/CI-side proof execution, while seed scope and shared-auth policy remain provider-owner decisions.
- **RF-008 — route Zen MiniMax rows through Anthropic:** Fixed in `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts`, `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts`, and `extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json`.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider`, provider catalog/routing, generated manifest metadata, and large-diff catalog seed review cost.
- Risk explanation: The compatibility risk is that a provider-owned seed can drift from Zen if the upstream catalog changes before live discovery runs. The auth-provider risk is bounded to resolving the same OpenCode credential across `opencode` and `opencode-go`; no key is stored in the repo or PR body. The large diff is mostly generated manifest/catalog rows rather than a cross-cutting runtime refactor. The remaining proof risk is limited to successful `minimax-m2.7` execution with a valid Zen API key; the PR no longer advertises MiniMax on the previously failing OpenAI-compatible route.
- Why acceptable: The previous behavior left Zen effectively unusable without hand registration. The patch restores the provider contract at its owner boundary, keeps live discovery as the preferred source when credentials are present, preserves a safe no-credential/no-discovery local seed, and fixes the concrete MiniMax routing defect surfaced by review.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec tsx \
```

```text
OpenCode Zen MiniMax M2.7 after-fix live proof
timestamp=2026-06-18T14:14:41.402Z
manifest_model=minimax-m2.7
manifest_api=anthropic-messages
manifest_baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen
request_url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/messages
request_headers=content-type: application/json; anthropic-version: 2023-06-01; x-api-key: [REDACTED]
```

```text
OpenCode Zen live proof at 2026-06-13T03:27:09.724Z
credential=present (redacted)
authenticated_discovery_models=48
authenticated_discovery_request url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models authorization_header=present status=200
shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48
live_request family=Claude model=claude-haiku-4-5 api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=claude-haiku-4-5 stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"

live_request family=Gemini model=gemini-3-flash api=google-generative-ai baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=gemini-3-flash stream=undefined max_tokens=undefined stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Qwen model=qwen3.6-plus api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=qwen3.6-plus stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
```

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` plus live evidence script `opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof.mts` stored in the evidence directory outside the repo worktree.
- Scenario locked in: Regression tests assert the Zen catalog exposes the 48 model rows, dynamic model resolution works for Zen IDs, shared `opencode-go` credentials populate the `opencode` catalog path, and Claude/GPT/Gemini/Qwen/MiniMax families route to the expected adapter/base URL pairs.

```text
RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-92495

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 9 skipped (10)
Start at  22:13:11
Duration  160.88s
```

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — fix or remove MiniMax Zen rows:** Fixed by routing MiniMax Zen rows through `anthropic-messages` at `https://opencode.ai/zen` rather than removing provider-owned catalog rows. The provider-catalog resolver and manifest rows now agree for `minimax-m2.7`, `minimax-m2.5`, and `minimax-m3-free`.
- **RF-002 — redacted after-fix MiniMax live output:** Redacted after-fix request output is now included above. It shows the corrected Anthropic Messages endpoint and headers, but the local available OpenCode token returns `401 UNAUTHORIZED`, so this does not satisfy successful model-output proof.
- **RF-003 — stronger real behavior proof before merge:** The route-level defect is fixed and the old failed `minimax-m2.7` OpenAI-compatible route is no longer advertised. Successful `minimax-m2.7` live completion remains a proof boundary until run with a valid Zen API key or the remote proof job's secret.
- **RF-004 — failing selectable `minimax-m2.7` row:** The selectable row no longer points at the route that failed in the previous proof. It now points at the Anthropic-compatible route ClawSweeper requested.

- **RF-006 — shared auth compatibility:** Shared `opencode` / `opencode-go` credentials stay bounded to OpenCode catalog loading. The compatibility risk for existing hand-authored overrides remains disclosed in `## Merge risk`.

- **RF-008 — route Zen MiniMax rows through Anthropic:** Fixed in `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts`, `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts`, and `extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json`.

The patch restores the provider contract at its owner boundary, keeps live discovery as the preferred source when credentials are present, preserves a safe no-credential/no-discovery local seed, and fixes the concrete MiniMax routing defect surfaced by review.
